companies.name: Hale Nalu Properties
companies.description: Boutique resort property in Kailua. Monthly grounds maintenance contract since January 2024. Three acres of maintained gardens including pool area, main entrance, and guest pathways.

companies.name: Turtle Bay Resort
companies.description: Major resort on the North Shore. Seasonal landscape contracts for entrance gardens and oceanfront walkways.

companies.name: Waikiki Shores Hotel
companies.description: Mid-size hotel in Waikiki. Monthly common area and poolside landscaping maintenance.

deals.dealname: Hale Nalu Properties - Monthly Grounds Maintenance
deals.description: Annual grounds maintenance covering pool area, guest pathways, main entrance plantings, and irrigation upkeep. Renewed annually.

deals.dealname: Turtle Bay Resort - Winter 2025 Seasonal Refresh
deals.description: Seasonal planting refresh for resort entrance and oceanfront walkways. Removal of spent annuals, installation of winter-blooming varieties.

deals.dealname: Waikiki Shores Hotel - Common Area Grounds
deals.description: Monthly maintenance of hotel common areas including lobby entrance, pool deck planters, and guest walkways.

engagements.body: Walked the Kailua resort grounds with Darren. About 3 acres total. Pool area needs work, existing plantings are mostly non-native ornamentals that aren't holding up in the salt air. Darren wants a Hawaiian native plant palette, his wife grew up on the Big Island and loves hibiscus. Proposing monthly service with a full replanting phase in Q1. Irrigation system is older but functional, will need zone-by-zone assessment.
engagements.title: Initial property walkthrough

engagements.body: Quarterly check-in with Darren. Happy with the hibiscus along the main pathway. Wants to add plumeria near the pool deck before the holiday season. His daughter just started at UH Manoa, he mentioned wanting the front gardens looking extra sharp for a family celebration in November. Budget approved for holiday planting addition. Will schedule crew for late October.
engagements.title: Q3 review call

engagements.body: Zone 3 timer malfunction causing overwatering near guest parking lot. Notified Darren, parts ordered from Island Irrigation Supply. Repair scheduled next Tuesday. Crew hand-watering the new ti plants until fixed. Darren's birthday is Jun 8, last year I brought him a plate lunch from Rainbow Drive-In and he talked about it for weeks.
engagements.title: Irrigation zone 3 repair

engagements.body: Met with Darren on-site to finalize holiday planting. Going with red and white anthuriums around the lobby entrance, extra bird of paradise near the pool. He mentioned his neighborhood HOA in Lanikai has been looking for someone to do the common area landscaping, about 15 homes on a cul-de-sac. Said he'd be happy to make an introduction if PG or anyone we know does residential. Told him I'd keep it in mind. Need to order anthuriums from Aloha Plant Supply by next week.
engagements.title: Holiday planting finalized

engagements.body: On-site walkthrough with Karen to plan winter seasonal refresh. North entrance needs the spent ginger replaced, going with yellow heliconia. Karen wants the oceanfront path cleaned and mulched before the holiday rush. Crew scheduled for three full days late October.
engagements.title: Winter prep walkthrough

engagements.body: Brad called to discuss refreshing the pool deck planters for spring. Current crotons looking tired. Thinking tropical ferns this time. Budget TBD, waiting on hotel management approval.
engagements.title: 2026 spring planting discussion

engagements.body: Summer check-in call with Karen. Resort is busy with tourist season, grounds holding up well. The heliconia near the north entrance needs thinning before it overtakes the walkway. Karen mentioned her husband just retired from Pearl Harbor naval base and they're planning a trip to Japan in September. She asked about doing something special for the resort's 15th anniversary in August, maybe a Hawaiian native plant display near the entrance. Said she'd get budget approval. Karen always brings mochi from that bakery in Kaimuki when we do on-site meetings, best mochi on the island.
engagements.title: Summer season check-in

engagements.body: Year-end walkthrough with Karen at Turtle Bay. Oceanfront path mulching looks great, yellow heliconia from the winter refresh is starting to bloom. Karen is thrilled. She mentioned her son Kai is getting married in March at a North Shore venue and asked if we could consult on ceremony flowers. Told her PG doesn't do event floral but I'd ask around. She said the resort holiday party is December 18 and wants the entrance looking perfect by then. Ordered extra white ginger for the front gardens. Karen's favorite Mother's Day lei is tuberose, she wants to plant some near the spa entrance next spring.
engagements.title: Year-end property walkthrough

engagements.body: Called Brad about the spring planter refresh. Hotel management approved the tropical fern swap for the pool deck. Budget set at $4,200 for materials and labor. Brad mentioned he's retiring in February and his replacement will be Lisa Nakano from the Grand Wailea on Maui. Wants the lobby entrance looking sharp for a family photo on New Year's Day. His grandson Marcus just turned one and the whole Fujimoto family is flying in from the mainland. Asked me to make sure the front orchids are fresh. Brad has been easy to work with for five years, I'm going to miss him.
engagements.title: Spring planter refresh approval

engagements.body: Met Darren on site to review holiday plantings. Red anthuriums looking good around the lobby, bird of paradise by the pool filling in nicely. Darren's daughter Aiko finished her first semester at UH Manoa, he's already talking about her winter break plans. He's hosting the Lanikai neighborhood potluck December 21 and wants the resort entrance looking festive for any neighbors who drive by. Mentioned the HOA landscaping for his cul-de-sac again, says his neighbor is on the board and keeps asking for a referral. Told him I'd think about it. Ordered more poinsettias from Aloha Plant Supply for the guest walkway.
engagements.title: Holiday plantings review
